purpose: Evaluate whether a buyer should pursue, price, structure, and execute an acquisition of a target company, producing an Acquisition Strategy Report from the buyer perspective.
clarification_questions: Who is the buyer and what is its current business model?
Who is the target and what assets/businesses are included in the proposed transaction?
What is the buyer's primary acquisition objective: market expansion, technology/IP, customer base, cost synergy, vertical integration, financial return, or other?
Is there an indicative purchase price, valuation range, or deal structure already proposed?
What data is available: public filings, financial statements, CIM, management presentation, diligence materials, or only public information?

workflow.name: Transaction Context
workflow.objective: Define the transaction perimeter, parties, asset scope, proposed consideration, timing, and decision question.
analysis_tasks: Identify buyer, target, seller, transaction perimeter, geography, sector, deal status.
Summarize proposed terms if available: price, stake acquired, consideration, conditions, timeline.
Classify transaction type: strategic acquisition, tuck-in, platform acquisition, distressed acquisition, reverse merger/shell acquisition, asset deal, merger of equals.

workflow.name: Buyer Objective Definition
workflow.objective: Translate buyer's high-level intent into measurable acquisition objectives and decision criteria.
analysis_tasks: Define strategic rationale: growth, capability acquisition, market entry, customer access, vertical integration, cost synergy, capital market objective.
Set buyer-specific success metrics: revenue uplift, EBITDA improvement, IRR, payback, market share, capability gap closure.
Prioritize decision criteria and assign weights.

workflow.name: Target Business Quality
workflow.objective: Assess whether the target is fundamentally worth owning.
analysis_tasks: Analyze revenue quality, margin profile, customer concentration, retention, recurring vs one-off revenue, working capital intensity.
Assess management quality, operational resilience, product/service differentiation, litigation or compliance issues.
Score target business quality using evidence-backed criteria.

workflow.name: Industry & Competitive Position
workflow.objective: Evaluate target's external market context and competitive strength.
analysis_tasks: Determine industry growth, cyclicality, regulatory exposure, fragmentation, disruption risk.
Benchmark target against peers on scale, growth, margins, valuation, market positioning.
Identify competitive moat and threats.

workflow.name: Strategic Fit
workflow.objective: Assess fit between buyer and target, including commercial, operational, technology, geographic, and cultural fit.
analysis_tasks: Map buyer needs to target capabilities/assets.
Identify revenue synergies, cost synergies, cross-sell potential, integration complexity.
Assess conflicts: channel overlap, product cannibalization, culture, regulatory or customer issues.

workflow.name: Standalone Financial Analysis
workflow.objective: Build target standalone financial view before synergies.
analysis_tasks: Normalize historical financials and identify one-off items.
Analyze revenue growth, gross margin, EBITDA margin, FCF conversion, capex, working capital, leverage.
Create base/upside/downside standalone forecast.

workflow.name: Valuation & Purchase Price
workflow.objective: Estimate fair value and buyer's maximum supportable bid.
analysis_tasks: Run DCF, trading comps, precedent transactions, and premium analysis.
Compare indicative purchase price to valuation range.
Calculate buyer walk-away price under standalone and synergy-inclusive cases.

workflow.name: Synergies & Value Creation
workflow.objective: Quantify how buyer can create value after acquisition.
analysis_tasks: Estimate revenue synergies, cost synergies, tax/financing synergies, strategic optionality.
Estimate timing, probability, execution cost, dis-synergies, and leakage.
Create value creation bridge from purchase price to buyer value.

workflow.name: Deal Structure
workflow.objective: Recommend transaction form and key terms that protect buyer value.
analysis_tasks: Evaluate asset purchase vs stock purchase vs merger vs staged acquisition.
Assess consideration mix: cash, stock, seller note, earnout, contingent value rights.
Propose protections: reps/warranties, indemnities, escrow, closing conditions, MAC, regulatory conditions.

workflow.name: Financing & Capital Structure Impact
workflow.objective: Assess funding feasibility and impact on buyer balance sheet/shareholders.
analysis_tasks: Evaluate cash, debt, equity, hybrid financing availability.
Model leverage, liquidity, credit rating, covenant headroom.
Analyze EPS accretion/dilution and shareholder value impact where relevant.

workflow.name: Returns Analysis
workflow.objective: Determine whether the acquisition meets buyer return thresholds.
analysis_tasks: Calculate IRR, NPV, MOIC, payback period under base/upside/downside cases.
Run sensitivity to purchase price, synergy realization, growth, margin, exit multiple.
Compare to buyer hurdle rate and strategic threshold.

workflow.name: Due Diligence Findings
workflow.objective: Convert diligence evidence into decision-relevant issues.
analysis_tasks: Compile financial, legal, tax, commercial, operational, HR, technology, regulatory diligence findings.
Tag each issue as valuation adjustment, deal term protection, integration action, or walk-away risk.
Identify information gaps requiring further diligence.

workflow.name: Regulatory / Integration / Downside Risk
workflow.objective: Assess execution risk beyond financial attractiveness.
analysis_tasks: Analyze antitrust, foreign investment, industry licensing, sanctions, and other regulatory risks.
Assess integration risks: systems, people, customers, operations, culture.
Build downside case and risk mitigation plan.

workflow.name: Recommendation
analysis_tasks: Synthesize all modules into pursue / pursue with conditions / pause / reject recommendation.
State recommended bid range, deal structure, diligence priorities, negotiation position, and next steps.
Provide confidence score and key assumptions.

guardrails: Do not fabricate financials or transaction terms; mark unavailable data explicitly.
Separate facts, assumptions, and model outputs.
Cite source for every public-data claim when implemented with search/filing tools.
Do not give legal, tax, or investment advice as final professional advice; frame as analytical support.
If critical inputs are missing, produce a preliminary report with data gaps and required next steps.
